reviews.review_msg: Interesting content, but grading felt very strict. Midterm and final were lengthy, 37 short answer questions! Assignments were demanding, including one requiring collecting insects outdoors and bringing them to the lab, which was challenging in March! The online structure was very flawed as well and needs improving. I would avoid this course.

Gail is knowledgeable, but the online delivery needs improvement. Marking is harsh with unclear criteria. The midterm and final are both entirely short-answer format, requiring 150-200+ words per question to avoid losing marks. This becomes difficult given the volume of questions and the limited time provided. Probably better course in person. :/

Dr. Anderson is genuinely one of the best professors I've ever had. She's easily accessible through email and she explains the content extremely well and makes science palatable and understandable to social science students. As long as you attend lectures, you'll be fine. She's an extremely fair grader and is very clear about what she wants.

Gail is super knowledgeable and has had a really impressive career. But unfortunately, this class was quite painful. Although the lecture videos were engaging, there are so many side assignments/discussions to complete weekly. There was no midterm, but the final exam was 120 short answer questions to complete in 3 hours. Literally impossible!!!!!!!
Such an interesting course and a prof which makes it so easy to understand and follow along. Clear instructions, assignments to be completed weekly but it's manageable. Would recommend this prof!!!

lectures, readings, quizzes and assignments were all good/reflected the content. Good lecturer and knowledgeable. graded harshly without any feedback. The final was stressful, not enough time to complete, lengthy answers needed. it was open book, be prepared to know your stuff because you will not have time to look for in depth answers.

Each week had lecture videos (1hr+ long) and weekly quizzes that were ok (sometimes very specific and other times very easy). The TAs mark kinda hard; sometimes arbitrarily deducting marks as they expect very specific things without clarifying it. Final exam was STRESSFUL- so much writing. While it was open book, it was hard bc of the detailed Qs.

Anderson's weekly lectures are quite lengthy and contain a lot of information which was very time consuming. The assignments and quizzes aren't too bad, however the written part of the final was quite hard and long. She expects multi paragraph answers to so many questions under a time constraint. Prepare for lots of writing in this class.

Best prof at SFU. When it comes to entomology Dr. Anderson is probably the most knowledgeable person in all of Canada. Really interesting hearing her stories of real cases. Labs are easy, mostly just identifying bugs with a microscope with detailed step-by-step instructions for every assignment. Exams are tough but are online, take-home, open book.
Awesome prof, she really loves what she does and her lectures are easy to follow.
